decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to use the card Balancing Act in combination with cards like Lion's Eye Diamond and Burning Wish to quickly put your opponent at a disadvantage by balancing the board state. The deck aims to control the game through card advantage and disruption, while also utilizing combo pieces like Anurid Brushhopper and Roar of the Wurm to put pressure on the opponent. By efficiently managing resources and timing your plays correctly, you can set up powerful combo turns to win the game.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with efficient mana ramping and creature generation, utilizing cards like Fyndhorn Elves, Llanowar Elves, and Priest of Titania to accelerate into powerful threats such as Deranged Hermit and Multani's Acolyte. The deck also includes strong utility creatures like Quirion Ranger and Wirewood Symbiote to protect key pieces and maintain tempo. Survival of the Fittest acts as both a tutor and graveyard enabler, ensuring the deck can consistently assemble its combos and outpace the opponent in resources.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with various Elf creatures and Gaea's Cradle to power out large threats like Deranged Hermit and Masticore. It also includes cards like Survival of the Fittest to tutor for key creatures, as well as effective disruption in the form of Wirewood Symbiote and Thornscape Battlemage. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creature plays and quickly close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize creature abilities and card interactions to create a powerful combo that can lead to victory. By combining Shaman en-Kor, Task Force, and Daru Spiritualist with cards like Worthy Cause and Transmutation, players can generate a lot of value and potentially win the game in a single turn. The deck also includes protection in the form of Mother of Runes and Orim's Chant, as well as card draw with Sylvan Library. The mana base supports the deck's strategy and includes cards like Birds of Paradise and Living Wish for additional flexibility.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by using counterspells, removal, and stalling tactics such as Standstill. The key strategy involves keeping the opponent's threats in check while building up card advantage through cantrips and draw spells like Fact or Fiction. By efficiently managing resources and ensuring board control through cards like Wrath of God and Swords to Plowshares, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through incremental advantage. The mana base is optimized for consistency and allows for activation of utility lands like Mishra's Factory and Dust Bowl.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells and removal spells, while utilizing Standstill to generate card advantage and slow down the opponent. It also includes land destruction elements with Dust Bowl and Seal of Cleansing to disrupt the opponent's mana base. The deck aims to stabilize the board with powerful control elements like Decree of Justice and Wrath of God, and eventually win the game with efficient threats like Mishra's Factory and Faerie Conclave. Overall, the deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver opponents through careful resource management and strong control elements.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruption spells. By using cheap creatures like Nantuko Shade and Hypnotic Specter, along with efficient removal in the form of Smother and Snuff Out, the deck aims to apply constant pressure while keeping the opponent's hand empty. The inclusion of cards like Cursed Scroll and Phyrexian Arena provides additional card advantage and reach. Additionally, the deck utilizes Wasteland to target the opponent's lands and disrupt their mana base.

The main focus of this Mono Black Control deck in Premodern 1 vs 1 format is to disrupt the opponent's hand and board with cards like Ravenous Rats, Hypnotic Specter, and Duress, while also applying pressure with cards like The Rack and Mishra's Factory. The deck aims to control the game through hand disruption, removal spells like Smother and Diabolic Edict, and the burn damage from cards like Cursed Scroll and Cabal Pit. The strategy is to keep the opponent's resources limited while chipping away at their life total for the win.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through removal spells and card advantage while utilizing the powerful enchantment Oath of Druids to cheat creatures into play. The deck aims to ramp up its mana quickly with cards like Mox Diamond and Rampant Growth, then uses Replenish to bring back enchantments from the graveyard. It can win by either overwhelming the opponent with creatures like Terravore and Treetop Village, or by using cards like Opalescence and Pandemonium to turn its enchantments into creatures for a fast finish.

The main strategy of this deck is to control the game using various removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Pyrokinesis while setting up for a powerful win condition with Oath of Druids. By playing Oath of Druids, the deck can quickly summon powerful creatures like Bloodfire Colossus and overwhelm the opponent. Additionally, cards like Pyrostatic Pillar and Cursed Scroll provide disruption for the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes cards like Naturalize and Gaea's Blessing to deal with problematic enchantments and graveyard strategies. With a mix of control and combo elements, this deck aims to dictate the pace of the game and secure victory.

The deck focuses on quickly getting powerful creatures from the graveyard onto the battlefield using reanimation spells like Reanimate and Exhume. The key strategy is to discard big creatures like Akroma, Angel of Wrath and Phantom Nishoba using cards like Cabal Therapy and Careful Study, then bring them back with reanimation spells. The deck also includes ways to generate mana quickly with Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual to cast these spells early in the game. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful reanimated creatures before they can respond effectively.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells, aiming to win the game as quickly as possible. By efficiently managing resources and continually applying pressure, the goal is to reduce the opponent's life total to zero before they can stabilize. With a mix of direct damage spells, efficient creatures, and burn spells that can be activated repeatedly, this deck seeks to maintain tempo and force the opponent into a defensive position, ultimately securing a swift victory.

The main focus of this deck is to deal quick and direct damage to the opponent using aggressive low-cost creatures and burn spells. By putting pressure on the opponent early in the game, it aims to secure victory before the opponent can set up their defenses. Cards like Ball Lightning and Sulfuric Vortex help maintain constant pressure, while burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast provide reach to finish off the opponent. The deck aims to win swiftly and decisively through consistent and aggressive damage output.

The main idea of this deck is to use the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful threat that opponents will struggle to deal with. The deck focuses on protecting this combo with various counterspells and card draw spells, such as Accumulated Knowledge and Impulse. The goal is to assemble the combo quickly and disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining card advantage. The inclusion of Mishra's Factory provides additional creature threats, while cards like Opt and Gush help to ensure consistent draws and efficient use of resources.

The main objective of this deck is to assemble the Tinker combo quickly and efficiently, using cards like Phyrexian Devourer and Altar of Dementia. By utilizing various artifacts and mana acceleration cards, the deck aims to disrupt opponents with cards like Defense Grid and Tangle Wire while setting up the game-winning Fling combo. The deck includes a mix of lands that provide mana fixing and acceleration to ensure the combo can be executed smoothly. Overall, the deck focuses on quickly assembling the Tinker combo and disrupting opponents to secure a win.

The primary strategy of this deck is to control the opponent's plays while building a board presence with Zombie Infestation. This allows the player to generate a steady stream of Zombie tokens to pressure the opponent while disrupting their game plan with counterspells, removal spells, and hand disruption. The deck also utilizes card draw and card selection to find key pieces of the combo and maintain a consistent flow of resources. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent with resilient threats and a disruptive game plan.
